implications: Patient & Public Involvement: Strong evidence of co-design: PPI groups involved in developing review questions, recruitment pathways, survey questions, and workshop materials across all work packages.
Patient & Public Involvement: Explicit strategies for diverse recruitment including parent support groups, trusted advocates, interpreters, and BSL to reach diverse communities and CYP with SEND.

Patient & Public Involvement: Inclusivity embedded across lifecycle: PPI involved in design, conduct, analysis, and dissemination, with specific adjustments for communication needs and reasonable adjustments.
Patient & Public Involvement: Comprehensive budget for inclusion: specific line items for PPI vouchers, childcare, travel, interpreters, accessible room hire, and training for PPI members.
Patient & Public Involvement: Inclusive design features: use of easy read information, verbal explanations, interpreters, BSL, and wheelchair accessible rooms to support participation.
Patient & Public Involvement: Active engagement described: PPI groups co-produce materials and facilitate workshops, with clear feedback loops and involvement in data analysis.
